# Scry

> Open-source Android app and Python MCP server that together turn any
> ROS 2 robot into something you can debug by chatting with an AI agent.
> Phone is the thick client; robot just exposes its ROS 2 graph as
> Model Context Protocol tools. No cloud backend, no telemetry.

Maintained by Phaneron Robotics, Inc. Apache 2.0 license.

## Quick facts

- Android 9 (API 28) or newer.
- ROS 2 Humble, Iron, Jazzy, Kilted, or Rolling.
- DDS- and middleware-agnostic (uses rclpy).
- Bring your own AI key — OpenRouter, Claude, OpenAI, Gemini, or local
  Ollama for offline use.
- Around ninety-nine MCP tools, thirty-one of which require explicit
  user approval before they run.
- Phone and robot communicate directly over LAN — port 5339, HTTP and
  Server-Sent Events.

## Get started

- [Install the Android app](https://phaneron-robotics.github.io/scry-web/get-started/install-android/)
- [Install scry-connect on the robot](https://phaneron-robotics.github.io/scry-web/get-started/install-connect/)
- [Pair the phone and robot](https://phaneron-robotics.github.io/scry-web/get-started/pair/)
- [First debugging session](https://phaneron-robotics.github.io/scry-web/get-started/first-session/)

## Use Scry

- [Chat with the agent](https://phaneron-robotics.github.io/scry-web/use/chat/)
- [File attachments and images](https://phaneron-robotics.github.io/scry-web/use/attachments/)
- [Voice input](https://phaneron-robotics.github.io/scry-web/use/voice/)
- [Background monitors](https://phaneron-robotics.github.io/scry-web/use/monitors/)
- [Sending feedback](https://phaneron-robotics.github.io/scry-web/use/feedback/)

- Brand assets: https://github.com/phaneron-robotics/scry-brand
